text.title: DDD Implementation Examples
text.summary: Concrete Python examples: Order aggregate, Money/Address value objects, domain events, repository interface, anti-corruption layer.

example.note: Aggregate enforces all state-transition rules. No public setters. Events collected for post-commit dispatch.
frozen=True enforces immutability. Validation in __post_init__ makes invalid states unconstructable.
Interface is defined in domain. SQLAlchemy implementation lives in infrastructure. Domain is never imported from infrastructure.
External API terminology (availableUnits) is translated here. Domain code only sees the InventoryChecker interface it defined.

text.reference: Pre-commit script that fails if domain/ imports infrastructure libraries
import-linter contract for domain layer purity
LLM prompt template for aggregate modeling
